example.name: adding_new_section_to_page
example.scenario: Add a new "About the Brand" section to the HomePage that follows existing design patterns.
example.user_request: I want to add an about section to the home page with a heading, description text, and a CTA button.

step.approach: Check src/pages/HomePage.tsx for existing section structure. Review docs/05-styling-system.md for typography and spacing rules. Identify the section_heading pattern from 3_common_patterns.xml.
step.expected_outcome: Confirm use of section_heading, page_section, and cta_button patterns.

step.approach:
Compose
with page_section classes. Use section_heading pattern for the title with OrnamentalDivider. Use text-foreground for body, text-muted-foreground for secondary text. Use for CTA.
step.code:
// In src/pages/HomePage.tsx, add before existing sections:
The Art of Timeless Elegance
Each Mornii piece is crafted to celebrate life's most meaningful moments, blending heritage craftsmanship with contemporary design.
Discover Our Story
step.analysis: No new components created. All tokens, spacing, and typography follow established patterns.

example.completion: The new section uses existing page_section, section_heading, and cta_button patterns. All colors use CSS variables (text-foreground, text-muted-foreground). Typography follows the established scale (H2 at 36px/48px). Spacing uses Tailwind scale (py-20, mb-12, mb-8).

example.name: creating_new_component_from_composition
example.scenario: Create a new TestimonialCard component that combines glass panel, ornamental border, and star rating.
example.user_request: I need a testimonial card for the home page with a quote, author name, and star rating.

step.approach: Check if existing BaroqueCard can handle this via props. Determine that testimonial-specific layout (quote block, author row, stars) requires new structure. Confirm it will be reused (home page + potentially collection pages).

step.description: Create component using existing building blocks
step.approach: Extend BaroqueCard for ornamental border and golden glow. Use existing typography tokens for text styling. Compose UI components for star rating display.
step.analysis: Uses BaroqueCard as base (existing component). No new CSS created - relies on existing glass-panel, ornamental-border styles. Colors use tokens (text-foreground, text-accent, border-border). Properly typed with TypeScript interface. Uses React.forwardRef for extensibility.

example.completion: TestimonialCard is created by composing BaroqueCard + BaroqueCardContent. No new CSS or tokens introduced. Follows all established conventions.

example.name: fixing_visual_inconsistency
example.scenario: A new page uses hardcoded colors that don't match the design system, causing visual inconsistency.
example.user_request: The new landing page looks different from the rest of the site - colors and fonts don't match.

step.approach: Scan the page for hardcoded OKLCH values, custom font families, and non-standard spacing. Compare against src/tailwind.css tokens and docs/05-styling-system.md typography rules.
step.expected_outcome: List of specific inconsistencies to fix.

step.approach: Replace oklch(0.95 0.01 210) with text-foreground Replace oklch(0.60 0.11 78) with text-accent or text-gold Replace custom font-family with font-heading or font-body Replace hardcoded padding with Tailwind spacing scale

example.completion: All hardcoded values replaced with design system tokens. Visual consistency restored across the application.
